game_name: Earth 2150: Escape from the Blue Planet

design_philosophy.core_loop: Harvest resources from missions, decide whether to spend them locally or commit to evacuation fund, manage persistent main base across campaign while completing mission objectives
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource allocation: do you spend credits on immediate mission needs or commit to the global evacuation quota? Secondary tension between completing mission objectives and maximizing resource extraction
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command a planetary evacuation effort across a dying world, making strategic trade-offs between short-term survival and long-term escape as Earth's climate deteriorates
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Unconventional campaign victory condition—winning isn't about completing missions, but accumulating 1,000,000 credits (500,000 for Lunar Coalition) for evacuation. The campaign has a global time limit but missions can be approached in flexible order. Environmental progression (frozen world → thaw → desert → lava) creates dynamic weapon effectiveness and unit availability changes. Full 3D graphics were groundbreaking for RTS in 2000

difficulty.continues_system: Missions can be replayed; player can adjust approach after losses
difficulty.checkpointing: Main base persists across missions; units retained between missions count toward population limits. Spaceport auto-launches credits at 10,000 threshold, creating permanent loss mechanic
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency—player can choose mission order (when available), build units at main base vs mission sites, customize unit classes with chassis/weapons/commands, decide unit command sets per class, and shuffle credits between main base and spaceport

progression.upgrade_system: Research unlocks new unit classes and weapons; unit customization allows players to choose chassis, weapons, and command sets. Unit leveling system for deployed units
progression.unlock_mechanism: Missions unlock conditionally based on choices and win/loss outcomes. Environmental progression (climate changes) unlocks/disables unit types (navies retire when water evaporates, heat weapon effectiveness varies)
replayability_hooks: Skirmish mode allows single battles with goal to eliminate opposition
Unit customization with numerous chassis and weapon combinations
Mission branching on win/loss creates alternate paths through campaign
Two stand-alone expansion packs (The Moon Project, Lost Souls) with conventional mission-completion structure

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling via scripted terrain changes (climate progression from ice age to desert to volcanic wasteland)
News clips about evacuation program construction (cosmetic but reinforce theme)
Scripted events during missions (hacker capturing units, alliance breakdowns)
Mission objectives contextualized within evacuation effort
Persistent spaceport location serves as narrative anchor for escape goal

faction_indicators: Three playable factions implied (UCS, Lunar Coalition, ED)
danger_signals: Scripted enemy actions (hacker takeover of UCS units suggests espionage/sabotage mechanics)
Alliance system with potential betrayals mid-campaign
Enemy resource gathering races—cutting off transport links affects rebuilding capacity
environmental_storytelling: Progressively deteriorating Earth environment visible on mission selection screen
Lava flows and ash landscapes indicate planetary collapse
Ocean evaporation removes naval strategies mid-campaign
Climate zones shift from ice to heat, forcing tactical adaptation
Spaceport construction visible, reinforcing evacuation timeline pressure
